modality_rationale: Each cell of the deliverable heatmap is a comparison: spec X covers concern Y at depth Z. Per-concern completeness grades are mandatory; the structural take (3-tier vs. 4-tier) is itself a comparison; the system-level vs. per-spec grade tension is a comparison. This is not collect / synthesize / forecast.
tier_rationale: User explicitly requests deep treatment of vendor integration ("deeply consider the native integration requirements of each vendor"). The per-spec A vs. system-level B+ tension is dialectical and must be argued, not declared. Thirteen specs and five committed locus positions exceed light-tier budget.

atomic_items.question: Which platform-level decomposition correctly groups the 13 specs into engineering-coherent tiers (the user's proposed Mechanical / Coordination / Governance, or a different scheme)?
atomic_items.format: argued committed structure with per-spec self-location table

atomic_items.question: What is the methodology for grading completeness — what distinguishes an authoritative spec from a shape-correct spec, and what rubric grades a per-concern cell A through D?
atomic_items.format: explicit rubric with grade definitions and a worked example

atomic_items.question: What concerns live at the Core Runtime / Mechanical Substrate level and how completely is each addressed?
atomic_items.format: per-concern table with grade + cited evidence + gap classification

atomic_items.question: What concerns live at the Coordination level (signal ingress, durable async, deployment placement, ongoing-work objects) and how completely is each addressed?
atomic_items.format: per-concern table with grade + cited evidence + gap classification

atomic_items.question: What concerns live at the Governance level (acceptance, authority, decisions, review) and how completely is each addressed?
atomic_items.format: per-concern table with grade + cited evidence + gap classification

atomic_items.question: Where does the Semantic / Composition concern set live (authoring classifier, factory bundle export, ontology tools)? Is it a fourth tier or a sub-region of an existing tier?
atomic_items.format: argued committed answer with engagement of the three-tier counter-position
atomic_items.must_appear_in_section: 3 or 4-6 depending on drafter's renumbering choice

atomic_items.question: How is each vendor integrated, what is RAWR's integration-point ownership, and how does each integration measure against the 10-rule descriptor-first / standing-on-shoulders checklist?
atomic_items.format: per-vendor table (oRPC, Effect, Inngest, Bun/Elysia, OCLIF, Drizzle, HyperDX/OTel, Auth provider families, Deployment targets, MAWE provider, Factory Bundle TargetProfile, Authoring Classifier LLM) with grade + integration shape + zone-of-strength

atomic_items.question: Which silences in the corpus are conscious deferrals (named reserved seam + fixed owner + trigger condition) and which are real coverage gaps (no seam, no owner, no integration law)?
atomic_items.format: two-column classification table with rationale for each

atomic_items.question: What does the visual landscape look like — which cells of the per-tier × per-concern matrix are addressed, partially addressed, deferred, or absent?
atomic_items.format: ASCII or table-rendered heatmap with grade per cell + legend

atomic_items.question: What are the highest-priority next specifications RAWR should land to close the operational substrate, integration-law, and authority-emission gaps identified across the corpus?
atomic_items.format: ranked priority list with named owners + trigger conditions + acceptance criteria

required_section_headings: Methodology — Authoritative vs. Shape-Correct Specs and Grading Rubric
Level I — Core Runtime / Mechanical Substrate: Concerns and Coverage
Level II — Coordination: Concerns and Coverage
Level III — Governance: Concerns and Coverage
Vendor Integration Assessment — Standing on the Shoulders of Giants
Open Gaps and Recommended Next Specifications
section_heading_notes: The committed structural take is FOUR tiers (Core Runtime / Semantic-Composition / Coordination / Governance). Drafter may insert a 'Level IV — Semantic-Composition' H2 between sections 5 and 6 (renumbering downstream sections) OR fold Semantic-Composition coverage into sections 4-6 as appropriate sub-blocks. Either presentation is acceptable as long as: (a) the FOUR-tier structural take is defended in §3; (b) the Authoring Classifier and Factory Bundle Export concerns are visibly graded in the heatmap (§9); (c) the Semantic-Composition tier is not silently collapsed into Coordination or Governance.
frontmatter_policy: no YAML frontmatter on the final report; open with # title line

citation_policy.preferred_anchors: spec-analysis note ids (e.g. [[rawr-effect-runtime-realization-system-spec-analysis-authoritative]]); for in-text section references prefer §N or §N.M format with the spec name spelled out at first mention
citation_policy.forbidden_anchors: any link pointing to workspace artifacts (interim-*, digest-*, locus-interim-*, scaffold, comparisons, evidence-digest, draft-angles, source-tensions, corpus-critic-gaps); the locus-interim notes are workflow internals and must not appear as citations in the final prose

argumentative_engagement_requirements: §3 must defend the FOUR-tier choice against the steelman three-tier counter-position; the engagement must be visible (not buried).
§7 must defend universal-principle / bespoke-shapes against a universal-template reading.
§8 must defend the claim that three integration-law seams are open (Inngest connect-worker Effect Layer; agent-role WorkflowDispatcher emission rights; auth-specific compiler gates).
§9 should grade vendor integrations on TWO axes: 'used at strength' and 'integration-point cashed' — the Inngest case especially shows these can diverge.
Each engagement must name the counter-position before answering it; do not strawman.
corpus_disposition_rationale: Step 13 (gap-fetch) is a no-op for this run. The 13 source specs are local files; the 18 preserved vault notes (13 spec digests + 5 landscape locus interims) constitute the complete evidentiary corpus. No web search is appropriate.
